seoTitle: North Haven vs North Moonta — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between North Haven and North Moonta. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: North Haven vs North Moonta: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in North Haven and North Moonta.
heading: North Haven vs North Moonta — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between North Haven, South Australia and North Moonta, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: North Moonta has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to North Haven (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is North Haven or North Moonta safer from flooding?
faq.answer: North Haven has a flood risk score of 55/100, while North Moonta scores 23/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: North Haven scores 17/100 for bushfire risk. North Moonta scores 19/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in North Haven or North Moonta?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. North Moonta has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.
